name: employment-contract-templates
description: Create employment contracts, offer letters, and HR policy documents following legal best practices. Use when drafting employment agreements, creating HR policies, or standardizing employment documentation.
capabilities: Generate offer letters with position details, compensation, and benefits
Create employment agreements with confidentiality and IP assignment clauses
Draft employee handbook policies including PTO, holidays, and code of conduct
Include termination conditions and severance provisions
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged 23 patterns that are all false positives. The 'backtick execution' detections are markdown code block delimiters (```) in documentation, not shell commands. The 'hardcoded URLs' are reference links to government resources (SHRM, DOL, EEOC). The 'weak cryptography' and 'system reconnaissance' findings matched placeholder text like [X] in legal templates. This skill contains only markdown documentation templates with no executable code, network calls, or security risks.
valueStatement: Employment documentation requires legal precision and consistency. This skill provides templates and guidance for creating employment contracts, offer letters, and HR policies that follow best practices.
userTitle: Create employment contracts and HR documents
limitations: Templates are not legal advice and require qualified counsel review
Employment laws vary by jurisdiction and may require customization
Cannot provide jurisdiction-specific legal guidance without additional research
Does not replace consultation with licensed employment attorney

useCases.title: HR Professional Onboarding
useCases.description: HR managers creating standardized employment documentation for new hires across multiple departments.

useCases.title: Small Business Owner
useCases.description: Business owners without dedicated HR staff need templates for their first employees.

useCases.title: Legal Department Support
useCases.description: In-house counsel drafting employment agreements with consistent clause structure.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Offer Letter
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an offer letter for a Software Engineer position with a base salary of $120,000, remote work arrangement, and standard benefits including health insurance and 401(k).

promptTemplates.title: Employment Contract with IP Assignment
promptTemplates.prompt: Draft an employment agreement for a full-time developer including confidentiality obligations, IP assignment clause, 12-month non-compete, and at-will employment terms under California law.

promptTemplates.title: Employee Handbook Section
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate an employee handbook section covering paid time off policy with tiered accrual based on tenure, sick leave provisions, and observed holidays.

promptTemplates.title: Complete Onboarding Package
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete employment documentation package including offer letter, employment agreement with all standard clauses, and acknowledgment forms for a senior executive hire.

bestPractices: Always consult qualified legal counsel before using employment templates in your jurisdiction
Customize templates with specific company details, compensation, and role requirements
Ensure at-will employment language is included where applicable under state law
antiPatterns: Using generic templates without jurisdiction-specific legal review
Making verbal promises that could create implied employment contracts
Applying non-compete clauses in states where they are unenforceable

faq.question: Are these templates legally binding?
faq.answer: These templates provide standard contract language but are not legal advice. Employment laws vary by jurisdiction. Always consult qualified legal counsel before using any employment documents.

faq.question: Can I use these templates in any state or country?
faq.answer: Templates are based on US employment law conventions. Requirements vary significantly by state and country. Local legal review is essential before use.

faq.question: What is at-will employment?
faq.answer: At-will employment means either the employer or employee may terminate the employment relationship at any time, with or without cause or notice, subject to certain legal exceptions.

faq.question: Are non-compete agreements enforceable?
faq.answer: Non-compete enforceability varies significantly by jurisdiction. Some states like California generally prohibit them. Always consult local counsel before including non-compete clauses.

faq.question: Do I need separate NDAs if the employment contract has confidentiality clauses?
faq.answer: Often the confidentiality provisions in an employment agreement are sufficient. A separate NDA may be useful for contractors or when broader protection is needed.

faq.question: How often should employment templates be updated?
faq.answer: Review templates annually or when employment laws change. Significant legal updates, company policy changes, or jurisdictional moves should trigger immediate review.
